course_description:
Students are required to identify, analyse and develop strategies for organisational, industrial and environmental challenges and disasters using the theories, models and methods of issues management and crisis communication. Techniques applied include environmental scanning, issue and stakeholder mapping and crisis communication planning.

Issues management and crisis communication are two of the most compelling, challenging, and complex disciplines in strategic communication.

The disciplines of identifying and managing issues and planning for or responding to crises effectively are critical to building and maintaining stakeholder relationships and organisational reputations. These two areas of practice are related but quite separate. Therefore, this course is structured to first address the theories, concepts and practice of issues management; crisis communication is covered in the second part of the course.

To make the best possible use of this course, you must be intellectually curious about the challenges organisations face. ﾠCommunication professionals are committed consumers of news and other media. As you go about your daily life, be sure that you are observing issues, crises, and organisational responses closely. Apply your growing knowledge to understand, analyse, and interpret the interactions between issues, organisations and stakeholders. This knowledge needs to be exercised to grow, so be mindful of what is happening around you and use your expanding understanding of these disciplines as the lens through which to view current events.

course_aims: This course will developﾠyour knowledge of and capacity to apply the theories, models and practices of issues management and crisis communication. Learning outcomes will include the capacity

learning_outcomes.description: Identify the importance of stakeholders in issue and crisis management.
Identify the importance of understanding and using a range of media and communication tactics to plan for and achieve different outcomes.
Demonstrate an understanding of the concepts, theories and practice of issues management and crisis communication.
Apply the theories, methods and models of issues and crisis management to identify, track and respond to real life and simulated scenarios.
Identify and apply ethical standards of practice when dealing with issues management and crisis communication.

assessment_details.title: Crisis simulation
assessment_details.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assignment is about preparing a crisis simulation for a client of your choice, which can be a large private company, a government organisation, or a neighbourhood cafe. That is, you provide a detailed overview of a potential crisis to the company’s management, the CEO and the Board of Directors. Your goal is not to resolve the crisis but to provide a detailed (and imaginative but realistic) crisis that a client may face in the near future. Assessment details: Length: 1000 words plus tabs/figures Style: Report Additional details are provided on the Blackboard. Student use of AI in assessments Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submit via Turnitin on Blackboard.
TurnItIn Receipts:
Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using TurnItIn . Before submitting any assignments for this course you must ensure you have completed UQ's compulsory online Academic Integrity Tutorial.
When you successfully submit your assessment to TurnItIn you will see text confirming your submission is complete, before being redirected to your Assignment inbox. On this page you can:
View the name of the submitted file View date and time of the upload Resubmit your paper (if necessary) Download your submitted paper Download digital receipt.
If you cannot see your submission in your Assignment inbox you should regard your submission as unsuccessful. Students are responsible for retaining evidence of submission by the due date for all assessment items, in the required form (e.g. screenshot, email, photo, and an unaltered copy of submitted work).
If the submission was not successful:
Note the error message (preferably take a screenshot). Go to your assignment page and see if it is possible to submit again. If you cannot submit again email your course coordinator immediately with the assignment attached.
Please visit this webpage for further advice on how to submit your TurnItIn assignment .
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the notes about applying for an extension.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Crisis Analysis/case study
assessment_details.due_date: Presentations in Week 9 16/09/2024 Written report submission in Week 12 18/10/2024
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This is a two-part assignment. The goal of the assignment is to help students identify and critically analyse an organisational crisis. Based on theories and best practices learned in the class, students then provide recommendations on how to better respond to a similar crisis in future. In the first part, students will individually present a crisis case study for about 10 minutes. The presentation will provide an overview of the crisis, a description of the organisation, a crisis timeline, actions, and responses taken, media analysis, stakeholder impacts, lessons learned, and recommendations. Students should use a mix of scholarly references, media coverage, and organisational materials. This will occur in week 9 . In the second part, students will submit an essay incorporating the feedback from their fellow students and tutors. The essay should address the criteria as outlined in the assessment task and rubric. The task sheet/rubric for the essay is available on Blackboard. The second part of the assessment should be submitted via Turnitin on Blackboard in Week 12 . Assessment breakdown: 25% Presentation (10 mins) 35% paper (1500-word essay) Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Recording of Oral and Practical Assessment All presentations may be recorded for marking purposes via recording facilities available where the assessment takes place (eg. ECHO360, Zoom, camera device) Recordings will be retained by the School of Communication and Arts for at least 12 months from the release of the final grade for the course. Recordings will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed by authorised school staff for the purposes of: Moderation of marking; Provision of feedback to the student(s) recorded; and/or Re-marking following a successful re-mark application Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Week 1: Introduction, orientation Introduction to issues and crisis communication management: Theories, best practices, and case studies Learning outcomes: L01
Week 1: No tutorial
Week 2: Key terms Key terms of strategic communication, risk, crisis and issue management Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Week 2: Issues monitoring Discussion of the key terms and definitions, and what are issues - and why are they of "public interest" Learning outcomes: L01, L03
Week 3: Organisations and their stakeholders Stakeholder concepts, stakeholder analysis Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Week 3: Identifying Stakeholders Stakeholder identification and management, preparation for Assessment 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Week 4: Issue management & issue lifecycles Theoretical underpinnings and concepts Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Week 4: Stakeholder & Issue analysis Theoretical and conceptual reflections, preparation for Assessment 1 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Week 5: Crisis communication I Crisis identification, management, and recovery Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Week 5: Issue life cycles Exercises, case studies, and best practices in issue cycle monitoring and management. Submit Assessment 1 on Blackboard. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
Week 6: Crisis communication II Concepts and cases Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
Week 6: Crisis management Workshop & brainstorming Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
Week 7: Media in issues, crisis & risk management Explicating the role of media in crisis communication Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
Week 7: Media & media conference analysis Workshop and case studies: How to respond to media during a crisis? Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
Week 8: Framing and Agenda Setting Finding the key message & proactive issue management Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
Week 8: Framing and Agenda setting Workshop on media and crisis Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04
Week 9: Storytelling & case study planning Storytelling during crisis communication. Assessment 2 presentations this week for peer feedback Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Week 9: Assessment 2 presentations Assessment 2 presentations for peer feedback Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Mid semester break
Week 10: Crisis renewal and recovery Crisis Analysis: Effective and ineffective strategies for crisis management Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Week 10: Crisis renewal and recovery Case studies and analysis of effective crisis communication Learning outcomes: L03, L04, L05
Week 11: King's Birthday holiday
Week 12: Future of crisis communication in the age of AI How to gain trust and reputation with your crisis management. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Week 12: Future of crisis communication in the age of AI, Feedback, Q&As Last Q&As for assignment #2 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Week 13: Recap What have we learned in this course about issues and crisis management? Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Week 13: Future issues and crises Future issues and crises Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
